title: Preserve the ID parse source
scenario: Invalid IDs come from ParseIntError. Keeping that source error helps diagnostics without giving up the typed ParseUserError API.
instructions: Change InvalidId so it stores ParseIntError. Update parse_user to pass the parse error through map_err instead of discarding it, and expose it from Error::source.

hints.body: Tuple variants can carry the original error value.
map_err can pass a function or variant constructor when the types line up.
After the change, InvalidId should look like InvalidId(ParseIntError), and Error::source should return that inner error.

completionExplanation: The parse error now keeps its original source and exposes it through Error::source. That keeps the high-level error useful while preserving details for logging and debugging.
